content: We need to add v2 of the user endpoint. The response shape is changing — adding nested address object instead of flat fields.
I'll add /api/v2/users/{id} alongside /api/v1/users/{id}. The v1 endpoint stays unchanged for backward compatibility. The v2 response serializer uses an AddressSchema nested under user.address.
How long do we maintain v1?
Recommend a 6-month deprecation window after v2 launches. Add a Deprecation header to v1 responses now so clients know it's going away. Document the sunset date in the API changelog.
6 months is fine. What's the header format?
Use the IETF draft standard: `Deprecation: Sat, 01 Nov 2026 00:00:00 GMT` and `Sunset: Sat, 01 Nov 2026 00:00:00 GMT`. Both are standardized headers that API gateways and client libraries understand.
Done. Also add a Link header pointing to migration docs.

Added `Link: <https://docs.example.com/api/migration/v1-to-v2>; rel="deprecation"` to all v1 user endpoint responses. Added middleware to inject this header automatically so individual route handlers don't need to set it.
Good. Let's write the migration guide doc next.

Created docs/api/migration/v1-to-v2.md with: breaking changes list, field mapping table (flat → nested), code examples in Python and TypeScript, and the sunset date. Linked it from the API reference index.
